steps.title: Find the perpendicular slope
steps.content:
Line $k$ has slope $m$. Since line $l$ is perpendicular to line $k$, the slope of line $l$ is the negative reciprocal.
$$-1/m$$

steps.title: Use the shared point
steps.content:
Line $l$ passes through $(2,8)$, so its point-slope equation can use that point and the perpendicular slope.
$$y-8=(-1/m)(x-2)$$

steps.title: Evaluate the new point
steps.content:
For a point with $x=3$, substitute $3$ into the equation.
$$y-8=(-1/m)(3-2)$$
$$y=8-1/m$$
The point is $(3,8-1/m)$. The answer is A.
